items.title: Jail then deportation for man who reversed over pub patrons

children.content: A New Zealand national has been jailed for six years after he admitted to reversing into a crowd of people at a South Australian pub.

items.title: China accused of targeting Port Pirie and Hobart metal smelters

children.content: The SA treasurer claims a foreign government is undermining the profitability of the struggling Port Pirie metal smelter.

image.altText: An industrial site on the edge of a river, as seen from above.

items.title: Public servants asked to remove word 'harmful' about SA algal bloom, FOI reveals

children.content: Emails released under FOI laws and obtained by the SA Liberal Party show government workers were removing the word "harmful" from communications about the algal bloom in SA waters, with one team leader labelling it "bizarre".

image.altText: dead, dried up fishes on sand

items.title: SA's history preservation organisation faces questions over future

children.content: The National Trust of South Australia admits it is cash-poor and will need to improve its finances, amid disagreements with its regionally-based volunteers.

items.title: Calls for change after soccer club told it needs men's team to compete

children.content: Millicent United's women's side is flying this season, but without a men's team the club has been told they are unable to compete.

image.altText: Two women standing on a soccer pitch, holding a soccer ball.

items.title: Migrant delivery driver says assault claim was not taken seriously by SA Police

children.content: A migrant delivery driver who alleges he was assaulted while working in Adelaide's CBD says he was not taken seriously by SA Police, who told him the case had been closed before he had given a written statement.

image.altText: A man of Chinese heritage wears a jacket and stands next to a hedge

items.title: Thousands travel to outback Agfair despite fuel costs

children.content: Fuel costs have not stopped thousands of people from descending upon Broken Hill for the biannual Agfair.

image.altText: A young woman smiling at the camera holding a sheep close to her body

items.title: Police confirm identity of remains found in SA bushland

children.content: SA Police say remains found in bushland south of Adelaide a week ago have been identified as belonging to Jeff Mundy, who was last seen by his family five years ago.

image.altText: Two women and a man stand near a fence with a bouquet of flowers attached to it, with trees behind

items.title: The alternative classroom disengaged students are choosing to be in

children.content: Of the students who have attended the Alternative Suspension program, 67 per cent have received no further suspensions.

image.altText: Art work showing a girl student in a principle's office with her head downcast and scribbles above her head

items.title: SA government needs certainty on LIV Golf by October, premier says

children.content: The SA Premier says the government is "monitoring" the situation regarding LIV Golf closely, and would need a guarantee of its future in Adelaide by October.

image.altText: Three men smiling and walking, two of them in sportswear and one in a business suit

items.title: Remembering SA's pain and battles behind the Murray-Darling Basin Plan

children.content: The Murray-Darling Basin Plan is under review, but with submissions due to close today, we take a step back in time to remember when dried lake beds were turning acidic, dairy farmers were selling up, and there was talk of water carting for Adelaide.

image.altText: Close up of cracks in dried lakebed

items.title: Three die after suspected hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship

children.content: A suspected outbreak of hantavirus, which is spread by contact with rat and mice faeces, has killed three people on a cruise ship.

image.altText: A cruise ship on the open ocean with a smaller dinghy approaching.

items.title: A hacker built a tool to track police officers. Then he tried to warn them

children.content: A security flaw means the location of devices used by police, potentially including undercover officers and those travelling home, is visible to anyone with a phone or laptop.

image.altText: A man wearing a dark hoodie is silhouetted against the lights of a police station with police cars in front.

items.title: Daughter hopes $1m reward will solve mystery of mother's disappearance

children.content: Marion Barter has not been seen since 1997. Her daughter is determined to find out what happened to her mother, who she believes may have found herself "in a situation she did not fully control".

image.altText: Woman with dark hair poses with two young children by her side

items.title: Australian businesses falling victim to new type of fraud

children.content: Higher inflation, a war in the Middle East, and a ban on surcharges are crippling small businesses. Now they are falling victim to a modern crime.

items.title: It was a record race time. Moments later spectators were suspicious

children.content: Tasmania's top horse race was run 37 metres shorter than advertised. Officials knew the track distance was likely incorrect but proceeded anyway. This is what happened.

items.title: Rice is cheap, easy to cook and the main ingredient in these four meals

children.content: Rice doesn't need to play second fiddle. The cheap and simple pantry staple can hold up a whole meal.

items.title: Traffic gridlock as park visitors turn out to see the turning of the fagus

items.title: Worker shortage sees people struggle in 'most extreme form of homelessness'

children.content: Victoria needs almost five times as many outreach field workers to support people who are sleeping rough, according to a study by the Council to Homeless Persons.
